exploration_log.aha_moment: Info.Get receives the raw decimal value and immediately reads value.Scale - adding Math.Abs(value) at the top of Info.Get ensures the sign metadata doesn't influence any downstream code path.
exploration_log.hindsight: The existing GetMantissa already stripped the sign from the mantissa bits; the fix is at the Info.Get entry point to be explicitly symmetric for both scale reading and mantissa calculation.

alternatives: strip sign in IsValid before calling Info.Get
key_decisions.decision: Add Math.Abs at the top of Info.Get
key_decisions.reasoning: Info.Get is the self-contained calculation helper; patching it at the entry ensures all paths within it (scale, precision, trailing-zero trim) work on the unsigned value

search_sequence.reasoning: Task explicitly names this file as the only target
search_sequence.result: Found Info.Get() nested record struct that computes scale and precision; GetMantissa strips sign from mantissa bits but value.Scale is read before GetMantissa is called

queries.query_text: Strip sign from negative decimal before computing precision and scale in PrecisionScaleValidator.cs Info.Get

solve_notes: Added `value = Math.Abs(value)` at the top of `Info.Get` so that negative decimals are processed as their absolute value before scale is read from `value.Scale` and before the mantissa is extracted. This ensures symmetrical behavior for negative values like `-0.001m`.
task_text:
The `PrecisionScaleValidator` in `Validators/PrecisionScaleValidator.cs`
calculates precision and scale using `Math.Truncate` and string-length
arithmetic. For negative decimals very close to zero (e.g., `-0.001`),
the truncation logic can produce an off-by-one scale count because the
minus sign is included in the digit count. Fix the precision/scale
calculation to strip the sign before measuring digits.
